title: [lichen_l2.c:806] lichen_l2_flags returns flags without checking interface state
description: Function: lichen_l2_flags (static L2 callback). Precondition: The function ignores the iface parameter (ARG_UNUSED) and returns fixed flags without checking if the interface is initialized. If called on a failed-init interface, it would still report NET_L2_MULTICAST. Zephyr's net stack probably doesn't call l2->flags() for failed interfaces, but this differs from lichen_l2_send/lichen_l2_enable which check iface_init_failed. Consistency would suggest checking here too. Priority 3 - minor inconsistency, unlikely to cause issues.
close_reason: Fixed: documented why it's safe to return flags without state check - flags describe static L2 capabilities (multicast support), not runtime state
